articles.title: Critical Zero-Day Actively Exploited in Ivanti Connect Secure VPN Appliances
articles.summary: A critical zero-day vulnerability has been discovered in Ivanti Connect Secure VPN appliances, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. Threat actors believed to be state-sponsored are actively exploiting this flaw to establish persistence inside corporate networks. Ivanti has released an emergency patch and strongly recommends all customers apply it immediately alongside a full factory reset of affected devices.
articles.whyItMatters: VPN appliances are the front door to corporate networks. An unauthenticated RCE flaw means attackers can fully compromise an organisation without valid credentials. Because exploitation is active and attributed to nation-state actors, any unpatched device should be treated as already breached.

articles.title: Apache OFBiz RCE Flaw Added to CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalogue
articles.summary: A critical remote code execution vulnerability in Apache OFBiz (CVSS 9.8) has been confirmed as actively exploited and added to the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue. The flaw requires no authentication and could allow a complete server takeover. Organisations running OFBiz are urged to upgrade to the latest patched release immediately.
articles.whyItMatters: ERP systems hold the most sensitive business data — financials, HR records, and customer information. A CVSS 9.8 unauthenticated RCE flaw on a business-critical platform is as serious as vulnerabilities get. CISA's KEV listing means federal agencies must patch within days, and all organisations should treat it equally urgently.

articles.title: Medusa Ransomware Group Disrupts Services Across Multiple NHS Trusts in England
articles.summary: Several NHS Trusts in England were hit by a coordinated ransomware attack attributed to the Medusa group, disrupting patient appointment systems, internal communications, and clinical records access. The attackers claim to have exfiltrated over 1.5 TB of patient and staff data. NHS England has activated its major incident response protocol and is working with the NCSC to contain the breach.
articles.whyItMatters: Ransomware attacks on healthcare are life-threatening, not just financially damaging. Disrupted appointment systems and inaccessible patient records can delay critical care and put lives at risk. Exfiltrated patient data also triggers serious UK GDPR obligations and could result in significant regulatory fines.

articles.title: 50 Million Customer Records Exposed in Retailer Breach Traced to Third-Party API
articles.summary: A major European retailer disclosed a data breach affecting approximately 50 million customers after attackers exploited an unsecured third-party API endpoint. Exposed data includes names, email addresses, physical addresses, and hashed passwords. The breach went undetected for over three months, highlighting the risks of poor third-party security monitoring.
articles.whyItMatters: With 50 million records exposed and a three-month detection gap, this breach illustrates how third-party API risk is frequently underestimated in supply chain security assessments. Under GDPR, the retailer faces fines of up to 4% of global annual turnover, and affected customers are at elevated risk of phishing and credential stuffing attacks.

articles.title: LummaC3 Infostealer Uses Kernel-Level Evasion to Bypass Leading EDR Platforms
articles.summary: Researchers have identified LummaC3, a new infostealer variant that abuses legitimate kernel drivers to disable endpoint detection and response tools before executing its payload. The malware spreads through malvertising campaigns disguised as popular software installers. Once active, it silently exfiltrates browser credentials, session cookies, and cryptocurrency wallet data.
articles.whyItMatters: EDR bypass capabilities significantly raise the cost of defence. Stolen session cookies allow attackers to hijack authenticated sessions and bypass MFA entirely, meaning victims can be compromised even with strong passwords and two-factor authentication. The malvertising delivery method also means any user searching for software downloads could encounter it.

articles.title: Quishing Surge: QR Code Phishing Campaigns Targeting Corporate Microsoft 365 Users
articles.summary: Researchers have documented a significant increase in QR code phishing attacks, known as quishing, targeting enterprise Microsoft 365 users. Attackers embed malicious QR codes into PDF attachments sent from convincing corporate-looking senders. When scanned on a personal device, the codes redirect users to fake Microsoft login pages that capture credentials and MFA tokens in real time.
articles.whyItMatters: QR codes are much harder for email security gateways to analyse than traditional URLs, giving attackers an effective evasion method. The technique is particularly dangerous because employees often scan QR codes on personal phones with fewer corporate security controls, and real-time phishing proxies can capture MFA tokens before users notice anything is wrong.

articles.title: Misconfigured AWS S3 Bucket Exposes 200,000 Healthcare Patient Records
articles.summary: A publicly accessible Amazon Web Services S3 bucket belonging to a US healthcare provider was discovered by a security researcher, exposing over 200,000 patient records including names, dates of birth, diagnoses, and insurance details. The bucket has since been secured, but it is unknown how long the data was exposed or whether malicious actors accessed it before disclosure.
articles.whyItMatters: Cloud misconfigurations remain one of the most common and avoidable causes of data breaches. Healthcare records are among the most sensitive and regulated data types, with HIPAA violations carrying significant penalties. This incident is a reminder that cloud storage permissions must be reviewed regularly using CSPM tools, not just at initial deployment.

articles.title: NCSC Publishes Updated MFA Guidance, Recommends Phishing-Resistant Authentication Methods
articles.summary: The UK National Cyber Security Centre has released updated multi-factor authentication guidance for organisations, emphasising phishing-resistant methods such as hardware security keys (FIDO2/WebAuthn) and passkeys over SMS-based one-time passwords. SMS OTPs remain vulnerable to SIM-swapping, SS7 interception, and real-time phishing proxy attacks.
articles.whyItMatters: MFA is one of the most effective controls against account takeover, but not all MFA methods offer equal protection. Many organisations still rely on SMS OTPs that modern attacks can bypass. This NCSC guidance helps security teams choose authentication methods that genuinely resist current attack techniques like real-time phishing and SIM-swapping.
